title: Placental tissue compositions
abstract: Compositions and associated methods of preparing placental tissue products/compositions are disclosed together with methods of utilizing the placental tissue composition for delivery of a therapeutic agent.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A composition comprising an ex-vivo amnion treated with a processing agent, wherein the processing agent comprises any one of an acid, a base, an anionic detergent, an ionic detergent, a Zwitterionsc detergent, an alcohol, an oxidizing agent, hypertonic solution, hypotonic solution, a solvent, a supercritical fluid, and combinations tliereof. 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein the oxidizing agent comprises any one of hydrogen peroxide and peracetic acid. 3. The composition of any one of claims 1 -2, wherein a concentration of the processing agent may be up to 10% weight by volume of the amnion. 4. The composition of any one of claims 1-3, wherein a loading capacity of the processed amnion is at least 1.2 times greater than the loading capacity of the amnion that has not been exposed to the processing agent. 5. The composition of any one of claims 1 -4, wherein at least one collagen-containing layer of the amnion is expanded. 6. The composition of any one of claims 1-5, wherein an epithelial layer of the amnion is perforated. 7. The composition of any one of claims 1 -6, wherein the amnion comprises a therapeutic agent comprising small molecules, proteins, cytokines, growth factors, and/or cells. 8. The composition of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the amnion is a sheet. 9. The composition of any one of claims 1-7, wherein a size of amnion is between about 1 iinr and 10 mnr. 10. The composition of any one of claims 1-7, wherein a size of amnion is between about 10 mm 2 and 1000 cm 2 . 1 1. The composition of any one of claims 1-10, further comprising a therapeutic agent. 12. The composition of claim 11, wherein the therapeutic agent comprises any one of small molecules, proteins, cytokines, growth factors, gene therapy agents, cells, and combinations thereof. 13. The composition of claim 12, wherein the therapeutic agent comprises antibiotics. 14. The composition of claim 12, wherein the therapeutic agent comprises analgesics. 15. The composition of claim 12, wherein the therapeutic agent comprises acellular placental products. 16. A method for enhancing a loading capacity of an amnion comprising: contacting an amnion with a processing agent, wherein the processing agent comprises any one of an acid, a base, an anionic detergent, an ionic detergent, a Zwitterionic detergent, an alcohol, an oxidizing agent, hypertonic solution, hypotonic solution, a solvent, a supercritical fluid, and combinations thereof. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the contact step comprises submerging, rinsing, perfusing, and/or spraying the amnion. 18. The method of any one of claims 16-17, wherein the oxidizing agent comprises hydrogen peroxide and/or peracetic acid. 19. The method of any one of claims 16-18, wherein a concentration of the oxidizing agent is up to 10% weight by volume of the amnion. 20. The method of any one of claims 16-19, further comprising agitating and/or sonicating the amnion. 21. The method of any one of claims 16-20, further comprising pulverizing, grinding, chopping, or micronizing the amnion. 22. A method for treating a condition with an amnion comprising: loading a processed amnion with a therapeutic agent, wherein the therapeutic agent comprises any one of small molecules, proteins, cytokines, growth factors, gene therapy agents, cells, and combinations thereof; and placing the loaded amnion at a treatment location of a patient. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the processed amnion releases the therapeutic agent at a controlled rate. 24. The method of any one of claims 22-23, wherein the placing comprises injecting, covering, packing, or enclosing the amnion at the treatment location. 25. The method of any one of claims 22-24, wherein the conditions treated compri se wound healing, osteoarthritis, and/or pain management.
